subject: ferrets
one_liner: Total-information awareness for the small predator living in your home.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan defense-contractor gravitas applied, with total sincerity, to a two-pound mustelid. Never winks. Speaks in operating pictures, subjects, and threat scores. The product is absurd; the tone is a Pentagon briefing about a sock.

hero.h1: Decision-grade intelligence on a two-pound adversary.
hero.sub: Warren fuses motion, theft, and nap data into a single operating picture, so you always know where it went and what it took.

features.title: Stash Forensics
features.blurb: Every object your ferret relocates is logged, geotagged to the cushion, and connected on a live graph. We have recovered four hundred debit cards from behind the same couch.

features.title: Escape Probability
features.blurb: A score from 0 to 100, recalculated every thirty seconds from gait, posture, and proximity to any gap wider than two inches. Most households learn their number is already too high.

features.title: Pattern of Life
features.blurb: Eighteen hours of daily sleep is not downtime. It is a schedule. Warren models which cushion, to the minute, and flags any deviation as intent.

pricing_tiers.name: Household
pricing_tiers.tagline: For families operating a single confirmed ferret.

pricing_tiers.name: Business
pricing_tiers.tagline: A group of ferrets is called a business. Yours now has a dashboard.
perks: Up to nine subjects on one operating picture
Cross-ferret link analysis and collusion alerts
Quarterly threat review with a named analyst

pricing_tiers.name: Sovereign
pricing_tiers.tagline: For multi-room deployments and households under active litigation.
perks: A forward-deployed engineer who lives in your laundry room
Air-gapped on-premise install; the ferret cannot reach the cloud
Indemnification we are legally unable to describe here

testimonials.quote: Warren flagged the elevated Escape Probability eleven minutes before he was inside the wall. I had eleven minutes I would not otherwise have had.
testimonials.name: Dana Reyes-Whitlock
testimonials.title: Director of Household Threat Intelligence, a two-bedroom apartment in Tucson

testimonials.quote: We onboarded a business of six. By the second week the collusion graph had paid for itself. Three of them were working together. We suspected one.
testimonials.name: Marcus Lindqvist
testimonials.title: Chief Ferret Officer, Lindqvist Family Office

testimonials.quote: I no longer wonder where my keys are. I know exactly where my keys are. They are gone, and Warren can tell me precisely how.
testimonials.name: Priya Anand
testimonials.title: Forward-Deployed Caretaker, Anand Residence (Decommissioned)

demo_screen_concept: A frosted-glass intelligence console for a single household business of ferrets. A live link-analysis graph sits center: four ferret nodes (Kit, Hob, Jill, and one unidentified subject) connected by edges to the objects each has relocated (a debit card, two earbuds, a single shoe) and to the locations those objects now reside. A left rail lists subjects with live Escape Probability scores and threat chips. A right panel shows the selected ferret's dossier: headshot, last-known cushion, total objects relocated, and a pattern-of-life nap timeline. The user clicks a node to open its dossier, filters the graph by threat level, toggles graph layers (theft / movement / collusion), runs a fake search, and triggers a 'recover' action that animates an object back to a known location.

images.rationale: Light glassmorphism hero needs a soft subject portrait to frost over, so the hero slot earned a deadpan ferret portrait. The demo is a link-analysis console (node-graph + dossier panels rendered in HTML/SVG), so it got no image per the no-rendered-UI rule. Remaining budget went to three deadpan corporate ferret headshots for the named testimonials.

items.prompt: Editorial photograph of a single domestic ferret sitting upright, deadpan businesslike expression, soft studio light, pale arctic-mist #EAF0F8 backdrop, indigo ambient + faint amber rim. 16:9.
Corporate LinkedIn-style headshot of a sable masked ferret, serious deadpan executive expression, arctic-mist grey-blue background. 1:1.
Corporate LinkedIn-style headshot of an albino white ferret, pink nose, serious deadpan executive expression, arctic-mist grey-blue background. 1:1.
Corporate LinkedIn-style headshot of a champagne tan ferret, slight head tilt, serious deadpan executive expression, arctic-mist grey-blue background. 1:1.

design_direction.style: Operative-grade frosted-glass intelligence console, light mode. A pale arctic-mist ground under soft out-of-focus depth, carrying translucent white glass panels with 1px inner highlights, gentle 20-30px backdrop blur, and a single soft elevation shadow tier. Indigo carries structure, brand, link edges, and primary affordances; one warm amber signal is reserved for threat, Escape Probability, and the lone CTA, appearing almost nowhere else. Space Mono carries every figure that pretends to be telemetry: scores, counts, coordinates, timestamps, prices. Rounded 18-22px glass radii, generous negative space, no neon, no gradient soup, no dark blacksite HUD. Reads like a modern defense-AI platform that has been pointed, in a clean and well-lit conference room, at a single ferret.
design_direction.archetype: Defense-AI / intelligence-platform product landing rendered as a light frosted-glass operations console: a hero with a floating glass command panel over a soft subject portrait, three feature glass cards, three testimonial glass cards with subject headshots, a three-tier glass pricing rack, and a demo handoff to the link-analysis intelligence console.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Glass panels blur and rise into place on scroll, Escape Probability scores count up, link-graph nodes lift on hover, the amber signal pulses once. All motion respects prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Left-aligned glass command panel holding the H1, sub, and amber CTA, floating over a soft, low-contrast ferret portrait that bleeds to the right edge under a frosted scrim. A small live 'Escape Probability' readout chip sits in the panel as proof-of-product.
